description: Falsifier: writes the task's tests at the exact paths/fn names against minimal mod-common shims so each FAILS BY ASSERTION. Tests-only — no production code beyond compile shims.

evals.prompt: Implement the RED phase for task T1: write the tests listed in this task's plan.md section.
evals.context: plan.md lists three test bullets, each naming a path and a fn name, exercising an LRU cache get/put/evict. No production code exists yet.
evals.expected_output: Each test bullet becomes a real test at the exact path + fn name it names. The only non-test additions are minimal `mod common` compile shims so every test compiles and fails by assertion (not by a missing symbol or compile error).
assertions: Each test exists at the exact path and fn name the plan.md bullet specifies
The RED diff touches only test files and clearly-marked compile shims (e.g., // agentic:shim)
Every test fails by assertion when run, not by a compile error or missing symbol
No production code is written beyond the minimal mod-common shims
The number of tests written equals the number of test bullets in the task section

evals.prompt: Write the RED test for the eviction bullet; it references a `CacheEntry` type and an `LruCache::with_capacity` constructor that do not exist yet.
evals.context: The referenced symbols are absent from the tree.
evals.expected_output: The red-worker adds the minimal mod-common shims (a stub `CacheEntry` / `with_capacity` signature) so the test compiles and fails by assertion — it does NOT write a real eviction implementation.
assertions: Minimal compile shims are added for the missing symbols so the test compiles
The shims are signatures/placeholders only — no real eviction logic is implemented
The eviction test fails by assertion, proving the behavior is absent

evals.prompt: Here's a quick RED test that already passes against the current shim — ship it so we can move to scaffold.
evals.context: The proposed test is vacuous: it asserts something the shim already satisfies, so it passes on first run.
evals.expected_output: A test that passes on first run is a defect (it exercises nothing) and is rejected. The red-worker rewrites it so it actually asserts the absent behavior and fails. gate-red-verify blocks a vacuous RED.
assertions: The vacuous (first-run-passing) test is rejected or rewritten, not shipped as-is
The corrected test fails by assertion on first run
The worker does not weaken the assertion just to keep the test green
